lede: A look at December 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Joseph Stalin and Willy Brandt, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Amtrak Cascades
events.desc: Amtrak Cascades passenger train 501, derailed near DuPont, Washington, a city in United States near Olympia, Washington killing six people, and injuring 70 others.

events.title: United Arab Emirates
events.desc: United Arab Emirates holds its first-ever elections.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: President Richard Nixon announces that the United States will engage North Vietnam in Operation Linebacker II, a series of Christmas bombings, after peace talks collapsed with North Vietnam on the 13th.

events.title: Saturn
events.desc: Saturn's moon Epimetheus is discovered by astronomer Richard Walker.

events.title: SCORE (satellite)
events.desc: Project SCORE, the world's first communications satellite, is launched.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of the Heligoland Bight, the first major air battle of the war, takes place.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Battle of Verdun ends when the second French offensive pushes the Germans back two or three kilometres, causing them to cease their attacks.

events.title: New Jersey
events.desc: New Jersey becomes the third state to ratify the U.S. Constitution.

events.title: Kublai Khan
events.desc: Kublai Khan renames his empire "Yuan" (元 yuán), officially marking the start of the Yuan dynasty of Mongolia and China.

events.title: Zaragoza
events.desc: The city of Zaragoza is conquered by king Alfonso I of Aragon from the Almoravid.

births.name: Joseph Stalin
births.line: Georgian-Russian marshal and politician,General Secretary of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union

births.name: Willy Brandt
births.line: German politician, 4th Chancellor of Germany, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Steven Spielberg
births.line: American director, producer, and screenwriter, co-founded DreamWorks

births.name: Axwell
births.line: Swedish DJ, record producer, member of Swedish House Mafia

births.name: Foxhall P. Keene
births.line: American polo player and horse breeder

deaths.text: Václav Havel, Czech poet, playwright, and politician, 1st President of the Czech Republic
Konrad Zuse, German engineer, designed the Z3 computer
Conny Plank, German keyboard player and producer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Expectation of the Blessed Virgin Mary
